description:
## Goal
`collect_diagnostics` reads `ModuleTypes::dead_branches` and emits `type/unreachable-arm` only when the dead branch's `BranchOrigin` is `User`. Synthetic Ifs (pattern-bind, fn-clause dispatch, param guards) are silent.

This is the ticket that unblocks destructure on every path — interp, JIT, AOT, repl — because all four share `run_frontend_gates_or_exit`.

## Depends on
- E1 (`BranchOrigin` tag).
- E2 (`dead_branches` consensus map).

## Behaviour change
- `is_synthetic_match_error_block` retired — superseded by the explicit `BranchOrigin::User` filter.
- The in-place `find_emptied_var` re-scan in `collect_diagnostics` retires too — it reads `dead_branches` instead.
- `unreachable-arm` warnings still fire on dead arms the user actually wrote (the `User` case in fixtures like `if_tail_call_in_arm_unnarrowed`).

## Out of scope
- The fold (E4) is what actually removes the dead blocks from codegen IR; this ticket only suppresses the noise.

## Done when
- After this ticket, the destructure probe (`{a,b} = pair()`, `[h|t] = xs`) compiles silently and runs on all four legs.
- Existing user-authored dead-arm warnings still fire (verified by `if_tail_call_in_arm_unnarrowed` and similar fixtures).
- `is_synthetic_match_error_block` is gone.
- All tests pass.
title: E3: diagnostics consume dead_branches + BranchOrigin filter (unblocks destructure)
